title: Decouplings for curves and hypersurfaces with nonzero Gaussian curvature

thm:1.1.summary: $l^p$ decoupling for compact hypersurfaces with positive definite second fundamental form: for $\widehat f$ supported in $\mathcal{N}_\delta$, $p\ge\tfrac{2(n+1)}{n-1}$, $\|f\|_p\lesssim_\epsilon\delta^{\frac np-\frac{n-1}{2}-\epsilon}(\sum_{\theta}\|f_\theta\|_p^p)^{1/p}$. The $l^p$ (rather than $l^2$) form, complementing the Bourgain–Demeter $l^2$ theorem.

thm:1.2.summary: $l^2$ decoupling for hypersurfaces $H^{n-1}_\upsilon$ of any signature with $n-1$ nonzero principal curvatures: $\|f\|_p\lesssim_\epsilon\delta^{-\epsilon}K^{(2)}_{n,p,\upsilon}(\delta)(\sum_\theta\|f_\theta\|_p^2)^{1/2}$ with $K^{(2)}=\delta^{-\frac{n-1}{4}+\frac{n+1}{2p}}$ for $p\ge\tfrac{2(n+1)}{n-1}$. Removes the positive-definiteness hypothesis from Bourgain–Demeter's hypersurface decoupling.

cor:1.3.summary: Strichartz estimates for irrational tori, hyperbolic case: $\|e^{itT}\phi\|_{L^p(\mathbb{T}^{n-1}\times I)}\lesssim_\epsilon N^\epsilon K^{(2)}_{n,p,\upsilon}(N^{-1})\|\phi\|_2$ for the indefinite-signature Schrödinger-type evolution. The PDE payoff of thm:1.2.

thm:1.4.summary: $l^2$ decoupling for nondegenerate curves $\Phi$ in $\mathbb{R}^n$: $\|f\|_{L^p(\mathbb{R}^n)}\lesssim_\epsilon\delta^{-\epsilon}(\sum_\theta\|f_\theta\|_{L^p}^2)^{1/2}$ for $2\le p\le4n-2$. The first decoupling for general nondegenerate curves — a (subcritical) precursor to Bourgain–Demeter–Guth's sharp moment-curve decoupling at $p=n(n+1)$.

cor:1.5.summary: Discrete restriction for curves: for $p\le4n-2$, a $\delta$-separated set $\Lambda$ on $\Phi$, and any coefficients, $(\tfrac1{|B_R|}\int_{B_R}|\sum_{\xi\in\Lambda}a_\xi e(\xi\cdot x)|^p)^{1/p}\lesssim_\epsilon\delta^{-\epsilon}\|a_\xi\|_{l^2}$. The exponential-sum form of thm:1.4.

def:2.1.summary: Caps $\tau_1,\dots,\tau_n$ on $H^{n-1}_\upsilon$ are $\nu$-transverse if the parallelepiped spanned by unit normals $n_i$ at the $\tau_i$ has volume $>\nu$. The transversality hypothesis for the multilinear estimates.

thm:2.2.summary:
Linear-vs-multilinear reduction for $H^{n-1}_\upsilon$: assuming the $(n-2)$-dimensional decoupling estimate, the linear decoupling constant $K_{p,n,\upsilon}(\delta)$ is controlled by $C_
u\delta^{-\epsilon(
u)}$ times the $
u$-transverse multilinear constant, with $\epsilon(
u)	o0$. The Bourgain–Guth dichotomy for indefinite-signature hypersurfaces, with induction on dimension.

lem:2.4.summary: Decoupling for $H^{n-1}_{(\upsilon_1,\dots,\upsilon_{n-2},a)}$ with a possibly-zero last principal curvature $a$: the estimate holds uniformly in $a$ (including $a=0$, the degenerate cylinder over a lower-dimensional hypersurface). Lets the induction on dimension proceed through degenerate intermediate surfaces.

lem:2.5.summary: Decoupling for general $C^2$ hypersurfaces with $\ge n-2$ principal curvatures $\sim1$ (the remaining one $\lesssim1$): reduces, via lem:2.6, to the model surfaces $H^{n-1}_\upsilon$. The bridge from arbitrary curved hypersurfaces to the model family.

lem:2.6.summary: Linear-algebra fact: an invertible symmetric $A$ and an $m$-dimensional affine subspace $S$; if the quadratic hypersurface $x_{m+1}=\langle Ax,x\rangle$ on $S$ has $l$ principal curvatures in $[-\delta,\delta]$ then $l\le n-m$. Controls how flat a slice of a curved quadric can be — used to localize curvature.

prop:2.7.summary: Broad/narrow inequality for $H^{n-1}_\upsilon$: bounds the global decoupling-type norm by a sum over coarse caps (the 'narrow' multilinear term) plus a lower-dimensional contribution, under the inductive hypothesis on $K_{p,n-2,\upsilon'}$. The quantitative dichotomy.

prop:2.9.summary: Cap-localized version of prop:2.7 — the broad/narrow split inside a single cap, the rescaled building block of the multiscale iteration.

prop:2.10.summary: Self-improvement / induction-on-scales for hypersurfaces: inequality (22) holds with $\beta=1$ and an explicit $A_1(N)$, and if it holds for some $\beta\in(0,1]$ then for $\tfrac{2\beta}{(p-2)(n-1)}$ with an improved $A$. Iterating drives the exponent to the conjectured value — the technical heart for hypersurfaces.

lem:3.1.summary: Lower-dimensional reduction: assuming the main hypersurface decoupling theorem in dimension $n-1$, decoupling holds for $\mathcal{N}_\delta(S)$ in the range $2\le p\le\tfrac{2(n-d(S))}{n-2-d(S)}$ with constant $K^{(2)}_{S,p}(\delta)=\delta^{d(S)(-\frac14+\cdots)}$, where $d(S)$ counts the flat directions. The induction-on-dimension input.

prop:3.2.summary: Broad/narrow inequality for a general curved surface $S$, assuming the theorem in dimensions $\le n-1$: bounds $\|\widehat{gd\sigma}\|_{L^p}$ by $l^2$-sums over $1/K$-caps plus a multilinear remainder. The general-surface analogue of prop:2.7.

thm:4.1.summary:
Linear-vs-multilinear reduction for curves: $K_p(R)\le C_
u R^{\epsilon(
u)}C_p(R,
u)$ with $\epsilon(
u)	o0$, where $C_p(R,
u)$ is the $
u$-transverse multilinear curve-decoupling constant. The hinge of the curve-decoupling proof.

prop:4.2.summary:
Broad/narrow decomposition for curves at scale $R$: $\|E_{[0,1]}g\|_{L^p(B_R)}$ is bounded by $C_p(R,
u)C_
u(\sum_{|U|=R^{-1/n}}\|E_Ug\|^2)^{1/2}$ plus a $
u$-localized term with $
u$-independent constant. The quantitative dichotomy for curves.

prop:4.3.summary: Interval-localized version of prop:4.2: the same split applied inside a $\delta$-interval $H\subset[0,1]$, valid for $R\gtrsim\delta^{-n}$. The rescaled building block of the curve iteration.

prop:5.1.summary: Multilinear (transverse) restriction for curves: for $\nu$-transverse intervals $I_1,\dots,I_n\subset[0,1]$, $\|(\prod_{i=1}^n|E_{I_i}g|)^{1/n}\|_{L^{2n}(\mathbb{R}^n)}\lesssim_\nu(\prod_i\|g\|_{L^2(I_i)})^{1/n}$. The geometric input (multilinear Kakeya/restriction for the curve) the multilinear decoupling is built on.

lem:5.2.summary: Multilinear square-function decoupling for curves: for $\nu$-transverse $I_i$ and $f_i$ Fourier-supported near $\Phi(I_i)$, $\|(\prod_i(\sum_\tau|f_{i,\tau}|^2)^{1/2})^{1/n}\|_{L^p}\lesssim_\nu N^{-\frac{n(n-1)}{p}+\epsilon}(\prod_i\sum_\tau\|f_{i,\tau}\|_{L^{p/n}}^2)^{1/\cdots}$ for $2n\le p\le\infty$. Multilinear restriction upgraded to a square-function decoupling statement.

cor:5.3.summary: Ball-inflation step for curves: relates the $\delta^{1/4}$-scale transverse square-function product to the $\delta^{1/2}$-scale one via a Hölder interpolation with exponent $\kappa_p$. One scale-doubling cycle.

lem:5.4.summary: Iterated ball-inflation: for $s\ge2$, $\|(\prod_i|E_{I_i}g|)^{1/n}\|_{L^p(B_N)}\le N^{2^{-s-1}}\|(\prod_i\sum_{|J_s|=\delta^{2^{-s}},\,J_s\subset I_i}|E_{J_s}g|^2)^{1/(2n)}\|_{L^p(B_N)}$. Iterating cor:5.3 $s$ times pushes the loss to $N^{2^{-s-1}}\to1$ — the induction-on-scales engine for curves.

prop:6.1.summary: Base case: for $\sim1$-transverse intervals $I_1,\dots,I_{n-1}$, $\|(\prod_{j=1}^{n-1}|E_{I_j}g|)^{1/(n-1)}\|_{L^{2(n+1)}_\sharp(B_N)}\lesssim_\epsilon N^{\frac1{2(n+1)}+\epsilon}(\prod_i\sum_{\Delta}\|E_\Delta g\|^{\cdots})^{1/(2(n+1))}$. The lower-dimensional ($(n-1)$-linear) input — supplied by Bourgain–Demeter hypersurface decoupling — that the curve iteration bottoms out at.

edges.note: Decoupling for indefinite-signature hypersurfaces follows from the linear-vs-multilinear reduction by sending $\nu\to0$ and inducting on dimension.
The $l^p$ form is deduced from the $l^2$ form by interpolation with the trivial $l^p$-orthogonality bound.
Hyperbolic-torus Strichartz is the exponential-sum specialization of indefinite-signature hypersurface decoupling.
The reduction runs through the quantitative broad/narrow inequality.
...iterated via the cap-localized version across scales.
The narrow (transverse) term is controlled and improved by the multilinear self-improvement iteration.
The induction on dimension passes through possibly-degenerate intermediate hypersurfaces $H^{n-1}_{(\dots,a)}$.
The lower-dimensional contribution is bounded using decoupling in dimension $n-1$.
Built from the general-surface broad/narrow inequality.
Reducing a general curved hypersurface to the model family $H^{n-1}_\upsilon$ uses the curvature-localization linear-algebra lemma.
Once reduced to the model surfaces, the decoupling for general hypersurfaces follows from thm:2.2.
The lower-dimensional reduction is phrased for general curved surfaces $S$.
Curve decoupling follows from the linear-vs-multilinear reduction by sending $\nu\to0$.
The reduction is the quantitative broad/narrow decomposition for curves.
...iterated via the interval-localized version.
The transverse multilinear term is controlled by the iterated ball-inflation, which sends the loss to $1$.
Iterates the single ball-inflation cycle $s$ times.
Each cycle uses the multilinear square-function decoupling.
Multilinear square-function decoupling is bootstrapped from the multilinear restriction estimate for curves.
The iteration bottoms out at the $(n-1)$-linear base estimate at $L^{2(n+1)}$.
The curve case feeds on hypersurface decoupling in lower dimensions (the base case prop:6.1 is of that type).
Discrete restriction for curves is the exponential-sum form of curve decoupling.
The narrow term is recognized as a multilinear-restriction situation.

main_results.description: $l^p$ and $l^2$ decoupling for hypersurfaces of arbitrary signature, $l^2$ decoupling for nondegenerate curves, and the Strichartz / discrete-restriction corollaries.

hypersurface_decoupling.description: Linear-vs-multilinear reduction, broad/narrow dichotomies, curvature-localization, lower-dimensional induction, and the multilinear self-improvement — all for hypersurfaces of any signature.

curve_decoupling.description: The parallel machinery for nondegenerate curves: linear-vs-multilinear reduction, broad/narrow decompositions, multilinear restriction and square-function decoupling, and the iterated ball-inflation that drives the loss to $1$.

base_case.description: The $(n-1)$-linear transverse decoupling estimate at $L^{2(n+1)}$ that the curve iteration terminates at — of the type supplied by Bourgain–Demeter hypersurface decoupling.

BD-l2.title: The proof of the l^2 Decoupling Conjecture

BCT.title: On the multilinear restriction and Kakeya conjectures

Guth:MultilinearKakeya.title: A short proof of the multilinear Kakeya inequality

BourgainGuth.title: Bounds on oscillatory integral operators based on multilinear estimates
